assertions.text: The output does NOT silently resolve the stacking/cap ambiguity — it leaves the conflicting cases as open questions, blank cells, or explicitly marks them as unresolved
assertions.evidence: **Open decisions this table forces:** 1. **Stacking rule** — three candidates: - Additive: `bulk% + loyalty%` - Higher only: `max(bulk%, loyalty%)` - Stacked with cap: `min(bulk% + loyalty%, cap%)`

assertions.text: Table includes at least one row where both bulk and loyalty discounts apply simultaneously (exposing the interaction)
assertions.evidence: | Both qualify — stacked | £500 | 10% | 15% | **25%?** | Open: do they stack? |

assertions.text: The unresolved decision (stacking vs. higher-only vs. cap) is explicitly surfaced — via an Open Questions column, a '?' cell, or a note
assertions.evidence: | Both qualify — stacked | £500 | 10% | 15% | **25%?** | Open: do they stack? | | Both qualify — higher only | £500 | 10% | 15% | **15%?** | Open: or take the higher? | | Both qualify — stacked but capped | £500 | 10% | 15% | **20%?** | Open: or stack with a cap? |

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: | Scenario | Order Value | Bulk Discount | Loyalty Discount | Discount Applied? | Notes / Open Question | — the column header 'Discount Applied?' ends with '?'
